title: Codex 2026 Prompting and Repository Practice · Contract Clinic: Codex Four Parts
summary: Apply current Codex guidance across CLI, IDE, app, planning, and repository instructions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt codex four parts increment in a Codex-specific prompt and AGENTS.md package. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use goal, context, constraints, and done conditions for material coding work.
Provide Claude Code repository guidance, verification commands, permissions, and durable project context.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Codex Four Parts
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt codex practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt codex practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Codex Four Parts contract.

content.text: Use goal, context, constraints, and done conditions for material coding work. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt codex practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Codex Four Parts, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a Codex-specific prompt and AGENTS.md package.

steps.title: Build Codex Four Parts evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-codex-four-parts-p63uc5" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Codex Four Parts into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-codex-four-parts-p63uc5
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Codex Four Parts model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Codex Four Parts applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use goal, context, constraints, and done conditions for material coding work.
